meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve stuck with basic apps (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Advanced SaaS Pricing Strategy Checklist
content.seo_description: Stop building basic apps. Master SaaS pricing strategies using Antigravity and Claude Code to increase client value and project profitability immediately.
content.answer_block: Stop charging hourly for basic apps. Transition to value-based SaaS pricing by integrating dynamic subscription tiers and usage-based billing directly into your architecture. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing schema and Claude Code to implement secure, automated stripe-integrated logic, ensuring your builds are premium, scalable, and highly profitable.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: another client asks for a 'simple' MVP, you grind through the boilerplate, deliver a functional app, and get paid a flat fee that barely covers your electricity. You are trapped in the 'basic app' loop, treating your expertise as a commodity while the real value—the recurring revenue potential—remains locked behind poor pricing architecture.

It is time to pivot from being an app builder to a SaaS architect. By embedding sophisticated pricing models directly into your codebase, you transform every project into a high-ticket asset. We are going to use Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your development workflow into a machine that ships enterprise-grade pricing logic in a fraction of the time.

steps.title: Define Tiered Value Architecture
steps.description: Map out your client's features into three distinct tiers: Basic, Pro, and Enterprise. This prevents scope creep and sets the stage for automated billing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a SaaS pricing database schema including plans, features, and usage limits. Use a relational structure that supports Stripe subscription objects.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate TypeScript interfaces for a multi-tier subscription model based on the schema provided by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Webhook Handlers
steps.description: Build resilient webhook listeners to handle subscription lifecycle events like trial expirations, upgrades, and churn.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust Express.js webhook handler for Stripe events, specifically handling customer.subscription.updated and invoice.payment_succeeded.
steps.claude_code_command: claude code --task 'Implement secure Stripe webhook validation and database sync logic in the current project.'

steps.title: Automate Usage-Based Billing
steps.description: Shift from fixed pricing to usage-based metrics to capture more value as the client's app grows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware function to track API usage and increment usage counters in Redis, mapped to specific user subscription IDs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Add Redis-based usage tracking middleware to the existing authentication flow.'

steps.title: Configure Dynamic Feature Gating
steps.description: Ensure your UI and backend logic enforce tier limits dynamically without hardcoding values.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a feature-flagging system that checks user subscription status against a required feature list before executing backend functions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude code --task 'Refactor existing API controllers to use a feature-gating decorator or middleware.'

steps.title: Deploy Pricing Analytics Dashboard
steps.description: Give your clients visibility into their MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue) directly from their admin panel.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a dashboard component query that aggregates subscription data from the database to display current MRR and user churn rates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create an admin dashboard visualization for Stripe subscription metrics using Recharts.'

pro_tips: Always implement a 'grace period' logic in your webhook handler to prevent immediate service disruption during payment failures.
Use Stripe's 'Tax' feature early; retrofitting tax compliance into an existing SaaS architecture is a nightmare for your clients.
Never hardcode tier limits in the frontend; always validate against the source of truth in your database to prevent bypass exploits.
Implement a 'grandfathering' flag in your database schema to protect early adopters when you inevitably raise your prices.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your pricing logic; edge cases like mid-cycle upgrades are where most SaaS apps fail.
content.next_steps: Audit your current client projects for pricing gaps, refactor one module using the steps above, and present the 'monetization upgrade' as a high-value upsell to your existing clients.
content.conversion_block: Stop building simple apps and start building high-value SaaS empires that pay you for years to come. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
